authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(52) — Goose Lake and tributaries (Modoc Co.)
sources.quotedText: (52) Goose Lake and tributaries (Modoc Co.), excluding Davis Creek (see subsection (b)(39)) and Pine Creek (see subsection (b)(106)). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: California Trout — Goose Lake Redband Trout species account
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Wikipedia — Goose Lake (Oregon–California), infobox coordinates sourced from USGS GNIS Feature ID 224325

parameters.note: Season runs from the Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last calendar day of the following February (Feb 28, or Feb 29 in leap years). Excludes Davis Creek (separate entry, 7.50(b)(39)) and Pine Creek (separate entry, 7.50(b)(106)).

regulations.humanSummary: Open (catch-and-release) from the Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last day of February
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 0-trout bag limit; all trout caught must be released, protecting the native Goose Lake redband trout population.

regulations.humanSummary: Catch-and-release only for trout; 0-trout bag limit

parameters.note: This entry excludes Davis Creek (governed separately at 14 CCR 7.50(b)(39)) and Pine Creek (governed separately at 14 CCR 7.50(b)(106)); those tributaries are not covered by this Goose Lake and tributaries rule.

regulations.humanSummary: Excludes Davis Creek and Pine Creek, which are regulated under separate table entries.
regulations.verbatimText: excluding Davis Creek (see subsection (b)(39)) and Pine Creek (see subsection (b)(106))

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
